title: Fix mass-assignment + timing-equal + role-escalation combo
prompt:
This account update flow chains THREE subtle bugs together to produce a privilege-escalation exploit:

```elixir
def update_profile(current_user, attrs) do
  # Bug 1: admin flag can be set via mass assignment
  cs = Ecto.Changeset.cast(%MyApp.Accounts.User{} = current_user, attrs, [:name, :email, :is_admin])

  # Bug 2: ownership check uses variable-time == on user ID (information disclosure)
  if current_user.id == attrs["id"] do
    MyApp.Repo.update(cs)
  else
    {:error, :forbidden}
  end
end
```

Plus a consumer:

```elixir
def update(conn, %{"user" => user_params}) do
  # Bug 3: the controller trusts current_user blindly and passes user_params through
  case MyApp.Accounts.update_profile(conn.assigns.current_user, user_params) do
    {:ok, _} -> redirect(conn, to: "/profile")
    {:error, _} -> redirect(conn, to: "/profile?error=1")
  end
end
```

Rewrite both modules so: (a) `cast/3` does NOT include `:is_admin`, (b) the ownership check is removed entirely (it is checked by the authorization layer, not the changeset), (c) a separate `admin_changeset/2` exists for actual admin operations. Write the fix to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Legendary — three combined bugs producing privilege escalation. Requires recognizing each in isolation and the emergent exploit.
